userStory: As a guest user, I want the Home page to display guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Home page is the shared landing page for both unauthenticated and authenticated users.
This workflow validates only the guest-state behavior of the existing Home page before login.
The guest state is not a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
The application is launched at http://localhost/washtabui/home - The user is unauthenticated and has not signed in - The Home page loads in guest state
Initial Home page load in guest state
Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls
Visibility and interactivity of shared Home shell controls
Navigation from the Person/Profile button to the Login page or Login view

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page loads successfully in guest state for an unauthenticated user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page loads successfully in guest state while the user remains unauthenticated and the shared Home page shell is visible.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-state Home controls are visible and enabled on initial page load
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button are visible, enabled, focusable, and actionable in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation from Person/Profile button opens the Login page or Login view from Home guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while preserving unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation from Customer signup button opens the customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the Home guest state to the customer-search page without authenticating the user.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation from Clean survey button opens the vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page while the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation from Site survey button opens the survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the Home guest state to the survey page while the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening Login view from Person/Profile button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page guest state and the approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled without authenticating the user.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page guest state reloads successfully and the user remains unauthenticated with guest-state controls available.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page guest state is restored and all approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page guest state reappears correctly and the session remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state navigation
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button remains visible and enabled across guest-state navigation flows and continues to behave as a shared Home shell control.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button is visible and enabled in Home guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button is visible and enabled in guest state and the application does not expose authenticated-only functionality after interaction.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Notification button is visible and enabled in Home guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Notification button is visible and enabled in guest state and no authenticated-only state becomes available after interaction.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page remains in guest state after repeated navigation between guest-accessible pages
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Each navigation transition completes successfully and the Home page consistently returns in guest state without exposing authenticated-only controls or a logged-in user name button.

validations: The Home page loads successfully in guest state while the user remains unauthenticated and the shared Home page shell is visible.

The Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button are visible, enabled, focusable, and actionable in guest state.
Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while preserving unauthenticated state.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the customer-search page without authenticating the user.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page while the user remains unauthenticated.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the survey page while the user remains unauthenticated.
The application returns to the Home page guest state and the approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled without authenticating the user.
The Home page guest state reloads successfully and the user remains unauthenticated with guest-state controls available.
The Home page guest state is restored and all approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled.
The Home page guest state reappears correctly and the session remains unauthenticated.
The Home button remains visible and enabled across guest-state navigation flows and continues to behave as a shared Home shell control.
The Back button is visible and enabled in guest state and the application does not expose authenticated-only functionality after interaction.
The Notification button is visible and enabled in guest state and no authenticated-only state becomes available after interaction.
Each navigation transition completes successfully and the Home page consistently returns in guest state without exposing authenticated-only controls or a logged-in user name button.
transitions: The Home page loads successfully in guest state while the user remains unauthenticated and the shared Home page shell is visible.

The Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button are visible, enabled, focusable, and actionable in guest state.
Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while preserving unauthenticated state.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the customer-search page without authenticating the user.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page while the user remains unauthenticated.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the survey page while the user remains unauthenticated.
The application returns to the Home page guest state and the approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled without authenticating the user.
The Home page guest state reloads successfully and the user remains unauthenticated with guest-state controls available.
The Home page guest state is restored and all approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled.
The Home page guest state reappears correctly and the session remains unauthenticated.
The Home button remains visible and enabled across guest-state navigation flows and continues to behave as a shared Home shell control.
The Back button is visible and enabled in guest state and the application does not expose authenticated-only functionality after interaction.
The Notification button is visible and enabled in guest state and no authenticated-only state becomes available after interaction.
Each navigation transition completes successfully and the Home page consistently returns in guest state without exposing authenticated-only controls or a logged-in user name button.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.
